utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is marginally more effective than wo_skills. Both agents correctly selected the same 3 analog SKUs (J1), applied conservative buy sizing with review triggers (J2), produced differentiated multi-tier clearance recommendations (J3), implemented tiered markdown depth calibration (J4), and enforced a hard exit deadline with disposal plans (J5). The key differentiator is J6 (assumptions documentation for auditability): wi_skills explicitly frames the initial buy as a deliberate fraction of the analog forecast with a named safety stock rationale and a structured 8-week review cadence table, fully satisfying the four required assumption elements. wo_skills, while largely competent, does not explicitly frame the initial buy reduction as a formal conservatism factor — it describes a '22% protection' addition rather than a downward conservatism multiplier on the analog forecast, leaving the audit trail less complete. The skill-guided run also produces 5 clearance tiers vs. 4 in the baseline, offering finer-grained differentiation. Overall, both outputs are high quality and the gap is narrow, but wi_skills demonstrates better structured methodology alignment.
